docs.title: Infrastructure Team Documentation
docs.text:
This site contains internal documentation, standards, and runbooks maintained by the Infrastructure team.

This is a living document. All content should be updated via pull request.

docs.title: What you’ll find here
docs.text:
• 📋 Standards – Baseline configurations and enterprise guidelines
  • 🔧 Runbooks – Step-by-step operational procedures
  • 🚀 Build & Automation – VM builds, templates, and pipelines
  • 🔐 Security & Compliance – CIS controls and hardening notes

docs.title: How to contribute
docs.text:
1. Edit or add Markdown files under the docs/ directory
  2. Submit a pull request
  3. Have another team member review and approve

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Onboarding
  • Patching Overview
  • Ivanti Security Controls

docs.title: Team Onboarding Guide
docs.text: This page outlines the basic resources and access needed for new Infrastructure team members.

docs.title: Required Access
docs.text:
New team members should request access to the following:

  • Active Directory admin groups (read-only initially)
  • Azure subscription (Reader role)
  • VMware vCenter (Read-only)
  • GitHub organization access
  • Monitoring platform dashboards

Access should follow least-privilege and be expanded as needed.

docs.title: Tools We Use
docs.text: Tool Purpose GitHub Source control & documentation Ansible AAP Configuration management Ivanti Security Controls Windows patching LogicMonitor Monitoring

docs.title: First Week Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Review VM build documentation
  • [ ] Walk through patching workflow
  • [ ] Review CIS baseline overview
  • [ ] Gain read-only production access

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: All production changes must be documented and reviewed. If something isn’t documented, assume it is not supported.

docs.title: Ivanti Security Controls
docs.text: This page documents how Ivanti Security Controls is used for Windows patching.

docs.title: Deployment Model
docs.text:
• Agentless scanning
  • Distribution servers per datacenter
  • Multiple consoles aligned to AD domains

docs.title: Patch Cycle
docs.text: Phase Description Scan Identify missing patches Review Validate patch applicability Deploy Patch during approved window Verify Confirm installation success

docs.title: Machines showing as offline
docs.text:
• Verify WinRM access
  • Confirm firewall rules
  • Check DNS resolution

docs.title: Patch failures
docs.text:
• Review patch logs on target host
  • Verify disk space
  • Confirm OS support status

docs.title: Logging
docs.text: Patch activity logs are stored:

docs.title: Windows Patching Overview
docs.text: This section documents how Windows patching is handled across environments.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This process applies to:

  • On-premises VMware VMs
  • Azure IaaS VMs
  • Multiple Active Directory domains

docs.title: Current Patching Approach
docs.text:
Patching is performed using Ivanti Security Controls in an agentless configuration.

Key characteristics: - Monthly patch cycles - Pre-production testing window - Change record required for production - Reboots managed via maintenance windows

docs.title: High-Level Flow
docs.text:
1. Scan target systems
  2. Review missing patches
  3. Stage patches
  4. Deploy during maintenance window
  5. Validate and close change

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text:
• Domain Controllers follow a separate schedule
  • Legacy OS versions may require manual intervention
